001package applicationMain;
002
003import java.util.ArrayList;
004import java.util.HashMap;
005import java.util.HashSet;
006import java.util.List;
007import java.util.Map;
008import java.util.Set;
009
010import entityClasses.Post;
011import entityClasses.PostList;
012import entityClasses.Reply;
013import entityClasses.ReplyList;
014
015public class GradingService {
016
017        // In-memory list of posts used to resolve reply targets and authors.
018        private final PostList postList;
019        // In-memory list of replies used to compute participation statistics.
020        private final ReplyList replyList;
021        // Role of the currently logged-in user for access-control checks.
022        private Role currentRole;
023
024        /**
025         * Builds a grading service around the shared post/reply stores.
026         *
027         * @param postList list of discussion posts used to resolve recipients
028         * @param replyList list of replies used to compute participation stats
029         * @param currentRole active caller role used for access checks
030         */
031        public GradingService(PostList postList, ReplyList replyList, Role currentRole) {
032                // Keep references so all grading operations use the same data sources.
033                this.postList = postList;
034                this.replyList = replyList;
035                // Store caller role to enforce instructor/grader/TA-only operations.
036                this.currentRole = currentRole;
037        }
038
039        /**
040         * Updates the active role used by protected grading methods.
041         *
042         * @param currentRole new role to apply for authorization
043         */
044        public void setCurrentRole(Role currentRole) {
045                // Allow controllers/tests to change active role without recreating service.
046                this.currentRole = currentRole;
047        }
048
049        /**
050         * Adds a reply while preserving who authored the original post.
051         * This relationship is required for rule-of-three analytics.
052         *
053         * @param replyId unique reply id
054         * @param postId target post id
055         * @param body reply content
056         * @param replierId author id for the replier
057         * @return saved reply
058         */
059        public Reply addReply(int replyId, int postId, String body, String replierId) {
060                // Reject missing/blank author to enforce input integrity.
061                if (isBlank(replierId)) {
062                        throw new IllegalArgumentException("Reply author is required.");
063                }
064                // Reject missing/blank message body to avoid empty participation entries.
065                if (isBlank(body)) {
066                        throw new IllegalArgumentException("Reply body is required.");
067                }
068
069                // Resolve the post being answered so we can preserve recipient identity.
070                Post targetPost = postList.readPostById(postId);
071                if (targetPost == null) {
072                        throw new IllegalArgumentException("Reply must reference an existing post.");
073                }
074
075                // Normalize user input before persisting.
076                String cleanBody = body.trim();
077                String cleanReplierId = replierId.trim();
078                // Store recipient author on the reply to support unique-recipient counting.
079                Reply reply = new Reply(replyId, postId, cleanBody, cleanReplierId, targetPost.getAuthorAsuUserId());
080                // Persist reply via ReplyList which also performs data-level validation.
081                String error = replyList.createReplyForPost(reply, postList);
082                if (error != null) {
083                        // Bubble persistence/validation errors as IllegalArgumentException.
084                        throw new IllegalArgumentException(error);
085                }
086                // Return the saved reply so caller can inspect stored values.
087                return reply;
088        }
089
090        /**
091         * Computes how many different students this student has replied to.
092         *
093         * @param studentId student id being evaluated
094         * @return number of unique recipients
095         * @throws AccessDeniedException when caller is not staff/instructor role
096         */
097        public int countUniqueStudentsAnswered(String studentId) throws AccessDeniedException {
098                // Only staff/instructors can inspect grading analytics.
099                requireStaffRole();
100                // Empty student IDs have no measurable participation.
101                if (isBlank(studentId)) {
102                        return 0;
103                }
104                // Trim to avoid whitespace-caused mismatches.
105                String cleanStudentId = studentId.trim();
106
107                // HashSet guarantees one entry per recipient ID, preventing duplicates.
108                Set<String> uniqueStudentIds = new HashSet<String>();
109                // Evaluate every reply and keep only those authored by this student.
110                for (Reply reply : replyList.readAllReplies()) {
111                        // Normalize reply author for safe equality checks.
112                        String replyAuthorId = trimOrEmpty(reply.getAuthorAsuUserId());
113                        if (!cleanStudentId.equals(replyAuthorId)) {
114                                // Skip replies written by other students.
115                                continue;
116                        }
117
118                        // Primary recipient source: original post author captured on the reply.
119                        String answeredStudentId = trimOrEmpty(reply.getOriginalPostAuthorAsuUserId());
120                        if (answeredStudentId.isEmpty()) {
121                                // Fallback for legacy replies: resolve recipient from referenced post.
122                                Post targetPost = postList.readPostById(reply.getPostId());
123                                if (targetPost != null) {
124                                        answeredStudentId = trimOrEmpty(targetPost.getAuthorAsuUserId());
125                                }
126                        }
127
128                        // Count only valid peer interactions (exclude blanks and self-replies).
129                        if (!answeredStudentId.isEmpty() && !cleanStudentId.equals(answeredStudentId)) {
130                                // Duplicate adds are ignored by HashSet automatically.
131                                uniqueStudentIds.add(answeredStudentId);
132                        }
133                }
134                // Set size is the unique-peer count used by Rule of Three.
135                return uniqueStudentIds.size();
136        }
137
138        /**
139         * Assignment-facing method name for the rule-of-three check.
140         *
141         * @param studentId student id being evaluated
142         * @return true if student has replied to at least three unique recipients
143         * @throws AccessDeniedException when caller is not staff/instructor role
144         */
145        public boolean checkGradingEligibility(String studentId) throws AccessDeniedException {
146                // Eligibility flips to true only at the threshold of 3 unique peers.
147                return countUniqueStudentsAnswered(studentId) >= 3;
148        }
149
150        /**
151         * Backward-compatible alias for rule-of-three eligibility.
152         *
153         * @param studentId student id being evaluated
154         * @return true when eligibility is met
155         * @throws AccessDeniedException when caller is not staff/instructor role
156         */
157        public boolean meetsThreeUniqueRequirement(String studentId) throws AccessDeniedException {
158                // Alias retained for readability/backward compatibility in callers.
159                return checkGradingEligibility(studentId);
160        }
161
162        /**
163         * Returns all replies authored by a student for quick reasonableness review.
164         *
165         * @param studentId student id being reviewed
166         * @return list of replies by the student
167         * @throws AccessDeniedException when caller is not staff/instructor role
168         */
169        public List<Reply> getAnswersForReview(String studentId) throws AccessDeniedException {
170                // Restrict review access to staff/instructors.
171                requireStaffRole();
172                // Output list for all replies written by the requested student.
173                List<Reply> answers = new ArrayList<Reply>();
174                // Empty student IDs have no reviewable replies.
175                if (isBlank(studentId)) {
176                        return answers;
177                }
178                // Normalize once and compare repeatedly.
179        String cleanStudentId = studentId.trim();
180
181                // Collect authored replies exactly matching the selected student.
182                for (Reply reply : replyList.readAllReplies()) {
183                        if (cleanStudentId.equals(trimOrEmpty(reply.getAuthorAsuUserId()))) {
184                                answers.add(reply);
185                        }
186                }
187                // Return evidence list used by gradebook proof panel.
188                return answers;
189        }
190
191        /**
192         * Builds a compact map of student id to unique-recipient count.
193         *
194         * @return stats map used by grader/teacher view
195         * @throws AccessDeniedException when caller is not staff/instructor role
196         */
197        public Map<String, Integer> showAllStats() throws AccessDeniedException {
198                // Restrict global analytics to staff/instructors.
199                requireStaffRole();
200                // Key: student ID, Value: unique peers answered by that student.
201                Map<String, Integer> stats = new HashMap<String, Integer>();
202                // Iterate all replies to discover which students have participated.
203                for (Reply reply : replyList.readAllReplies()) {
204                        // Normalize replier ID to avoid null/whitespace edge cases.
205                        String replier = trimOrEmpty(reply.getAuthorAsuUserId());
206                        if (replier.isEmpty()) {
207                                // Skip malformed replies with no author.
208                                continue;
209                        }
210                        if (stats.containsKey(replier)) {
211                                // Already computed this student's count; avoid redundant recalculation.
212                                continue;
213                        }
214                        // Compute Rule-of-Three numerator for each discovered student.
215                        stats.put(replier, countUniqueStudentsAnswered(replier));
216                }
217                // Return full snapshot used by teacher/staff stats views.
218                return stats;
219        }
220
221        /**
222         * Enforces that only instructional roles can access grading analytics.
223         *
224         * @throws AccessDeniedException when current role is not INSTRUCTOR, GRADER, or TA
225         */
226        private void requireStaffRole() throws AccessDeniedException {
227                // Permit only roles authorized to access grading analytics.
228                if (currentRole != Role.INSTRUCTOR && currentRole != Role.GRADER && currentRole != Role.TA) {
229                        // Student/other roles receive explicit access denial.
230                        throw new AccessDeniedException("Access denied. Staff role required.");
231                }
232        }
233
234        /**
235         * Checks whether a string is null or whitespace-only.
236         *
237         * @param value value to evaluate
238         * @return true when value is null or blank after trimming
239         */
240        private boolean isBlank(String value) {
241                // Utility: true when null or whitespace-only.
242                return value == null || value.trim().isEmpty();
243        }
244
245        /**
246         * Normalizes null strings to empty strings and trims surrounding spaces.
247         *
248         * @param value input value
249         * @return normalized non-null value
250         */
251        private String trimOrEmpty(String value) {
252                // Utility: normalize null to empty string for safe comparisons.
253                if (value == null) {
254                        return "";
255                }
256                // Utility: normalize surrounding whitespace.
257                return value.trim();
258        }
259}
